opportunity.title: Show HN: ScrapAI – We scrape 500 sites. AI runs once per site, not per page
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: I appreciate the architectural choice of batching inference at the site level. As someone who's built multi-agent systems, I'd note that this works well for static classification but can miss dynamic content that agents would need to re-evaluate per page. In agent architectures, we often need per-context inference because each page may have different entity relationships or navigation states that influence downstream agent decisions. Your approach is efficient for data extraction, but agent systems typically require more granular state tracking.
actions.reasoning: This touches on AI inference architecture and developer tooling, which allows for a genuine technical discussion about agent design trade-offs.
